title: Helenium leguiffei

preparations: Helenium amellae is actually the one known as "giant sneezeweed", but I'll assume you are asking about Helenium amellae's sister  Helenium leukenhoei,   not Helenium leguiffei,  which seems to be a non-existent species. intro: Helenium amellae, commonly known as sneezeweed, is a flowering plant species native to North America, known for its bright yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Helenium amellae include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
